wellCovered: Most API endpoint wiring is executed
Admin content mutation happy paths and representative auth/CSRF behavior
Public page/blog/work/site DTO projection, pagination, draft hiding, and search behavior
AI batch happy paths, auto-apply, partial failure, and persistence
WorkVideo local upload, confirm, delete, reorder, cleanup scheduling, HLS success, and public projection
misleadingSignals: 90.1% line coverage is inflated by thin endpoints, DTOs, module registration, and broad startup execution
Full coverage includes architecture tests and a skipped contract project
CustomWebApplicationFactory/TestAuthHandler proves endpoint authorization but not production cookie/OIDC events
DI/startup coverage can execute constructors/registrations without proving runtime failure behavior
Branch coverage is the better current planning signal

recommendedTests: Fake HttpMessageHandler OpenAI and Azure success
Single object and HLS prefix delete behavior
Confirm failure for missing object, wrong size or MIME, invalid MP4, stale version, repeated confirm
ffmpeg start failure, timeout, non-zero stderr, missing manifest, invalid ffprobe duration
Admin OIDC callback enriches claims/session properties
Non-admin callback revokes session and redirects
Storage failure returns 500 without orphan side effects
Search normalization for Korean, English, punctuation, whitespace, HTML and JSON
Malformed/non-object JSON and markdown/plain text extraction

doNotChase: Do not increase UnitTests coverage just to improve the number
Do not unit-test EF query behavior, auth middleware, routing, or storage side effects
Do not add a high global threshold yet
Do not treat WoongBlog.Api 99.3% line coverage as exhaustive endpoint behavior
validations: Ran npx skills find dotnet coverage test audit; no external skill installed
Inspected key per-class coverage HTML files
Used three subagents for independent review slices
recommendation: Treat the current suite as a solid baseline, not a finish line. Reinforce AI provider failures, WorkVideo R2/storage/HLS failures, production auth events, validation/proxy/security contracts, and media/search edge cases before adding coverage thresholds.
